deals.title: WordPress Site Build - Linens and More - Retainer
deals.description: Monthly website maintenance, hosting, and SEO updates

interactions.subject: Discovery Call
interactions.notes:
# SEO Strategy Call with Linens & More

## Action Items

- Email the client a detailed proposal covering Phase 1 implementation, pricing, and timeline.
- Include a separate section outlining requirements and cost estimate for rebuilding the Carriage Group B2B wholesale site (login, price masking, custom checkout, invoicing, shipping calculations).
- Attach the SEO audit report and a list of identified technical issues (metadata, H1 tags, missing blog, local GMB gaps).
- Follow up with the internal team to confirm resource availability for the proposed 60‑90 day rollout.
- Request any additional specifications the client has for payment options and account‑based pricing on the wholesale site.

## Business Overview

- Client runs *Linens and More* (consumer e‑commerce) and *Carriage Group* (B2B wholesale).
- Both sites have been live for about 12 months.
- Primary product categories: home décor, bedding, cushions, quilts.

## Current Challenges

- Low conversion rate on paid ads (≈ 0.41%).
- Monthly ad spend around *$200‑$300* with limited ROI.
- Economic slowdown affecting sales.
- SEO foundation weak: missing H1, poor metadata, no blog, limited local GMB info.
- Homepage lacks clear value proposition and brand story.

## Current Marketing Efforts

- Running Google Ads and Facebook Ads.
- Using Mailchimp for a weekly newsletter.
- No active SEO work beyond basic site setup.

## SEO Audit Findings

- Visibility for NZ home‑and‑lifestyle shoppers is low.
- Top keyword opportunities: “home décor NZ” (≈ 1,800 searches), “luxury bedding NZ”, “designer cushions Auckland”.
- No blog or inspirational content to capture informational traffic.
- Product pages contain only specs; lack storytelling and social proof.
- Email capture placed low in footer; high potential for pop‑up or above‑fold form.
- Google Business Profile missing description, categories, service locations, product listings, and high‑quality photos.

## Recommendations

- *Site Structure & Conversion*: rewrite homepage messaging, add clear CTAs, streamline navigation/checkout, implement aggressive email capture (pop‑up).
- *Content Strategy*: launch blog (4–8 posts/month), create inspiration pages, optimize product copy with benefits and storytelling.
- *Technical SEO*: fix H1‑H6 hierarchy, update metadata, add product and schema markup, improve site speed and mobile UX.
- *Link Building*: acquire external mentions to boost domain authority.
- *Local SEO*: complete Google Business Profile, add photos, encourage 4‑star+ reviews, post regular product updates.

## Proposed Phases & Pricing

- *Phase 1 (Conversion & Technical SEO)*: $4,000 total, 50 % ($2,000) due upfront, remainder after 60‑90 days upon client approval of results.
- *Phase 2 (Ongoing SEO)*
  - Growth Campaign: NZ$1,600/month (4 blog posts, link building, monthly audits, strategy calls).
  - Domination Campaign: NZ$2,400/month (double content output, accelerated authority building).

## Discussion Points

- Client interested in focusing on the Carriage Group B2B site rebuild rather than additional work on Linens and More.
- Need to incorporate wholesale‑specific features: account login, price masking, varied payment terms, custom freight calculations, integration with inventory system.
- Client confirmed ability to receive proposal via email and will review with their team.

---

End of notes.
